patterns_recognized: BAL@HOU recurs for the third time in the archive (7/17 lost 3-2 as sole GOLD CONFIRM, 7/18 correctly SKIPped at D-grade) with today's F5_bullpen_gap=+0.68 identical to 7/17's value. Today's stack is objectively stronger (grade A, all four dimensions affirm vs. 7/17's weak B(2) BvP-driven stack), but the pipeline's own confidence_downgrade=True plus stress_warnings=bullpen_fatigue;short_term_skill directly undercut the sole differentiating F-signal (bullpen quality) -- DOWNGRADE applied.

CIN@COL repeats with an identical F5_bullpen_gap=+1.77 fingerprint to 7/17, where the exact same edge-floor-blocked GOLD (edge=3.4pp, just under the 4pp minimum) won 7-2 and was flagged as the archive's leading candidate for relaxing the edge floor when F5_bullpen_gap>1.5. Today's version clears cleanly (no CAP1/CAP10, tier remains GOLD, grade B+) -- CONFIRMed rather than treated as a fresh SKIP.

TB@BOS again shows HARD CAP 7 firing on a sub-0.04 'not meaningful' bullpen gap (+0.0117), the identical value that suppressed this same matchup on 7/17 before BOS swept both games 10-0 and 5-3. Archive now has three-plus CAP-7 marginal-gap false negatives (7/10 KC@BAL, 7/12, 7/17 TB@BOS); noted but not overridden since the CSV's own tier column (not just the stake multiplier) already reads SKIP here, unlike the CLE@MIA/CIN@COL precedent where tier survives the cap.

D-grade picks with SP xERA and PQI both opposing the pick (TEX@ATL, PIT@CLE, SD@KC, MIN@CHC, DET@LAA, SF@SEA, STL@ARI) remain the most reliable SKIP-confirmation pattern in the July archive -- directional fade is real but OVERRIDE to the opposing side stays unwarranted per repeated 7/9-7/18 findings.

SF@SEA and MIA@MIL both carry explicit 'do not extrapolate' flags from recent postgames (7/17 SF 7-0 win was pure variance against every signal; MIA@MIL's CAP7+CAP10 stack has now appeared three straight days with one thin win and no resolved verdict) -- both CONFIRMed as-is without adjustment.

CHW @ TOR.reasoning: Bottom-bucket marginal favorite (pick_prob=0.500) with no F-signal firing and HARD CAP 10 correctly zeroing the score. Confirmed live via statsapi: Yesavage (TOR) vs Burke (CHW) as listed. Nothing in the signal stack or live context justifies elevation. CONFIRM SKIP.

LAD @ NYY.reasoning: Pre-cap stack is only F5_bullpen_gap=+0.98 and Stage agreement -- no F1/SP-xERA/PQI dimension at all. Per the 7/18 lesson, F5-only signals without F1 or F3 corroboration are insufficient for a GOLD stake (same conclusion reached on 7/17 and 7/18 LAD@NYY entries, both correctly downgraded). HARD CAP 10 already zeroed the score. CONFIRM SKIP.

TB @ BOS.reasoning: Pre-cap A- (SP edge 0.38 agrees, PQI +8.3 confirms, Stage agree) but no F-signal fires, and HARD CAP 7 knocks the score to B- on a hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap of only +0.0117 -- the identical marginal value that suppressed this same TB@BOS matchup on 7/17 (BOS then swept both games 10-0, 5-3). This is now a repeated CAP-7 false-negative fingerprint on this pairing, but the CSV's own tier column already reads SKIP outright (not just a zeroed stake), so I'm not overriding the label -- flagging for the ongoing CAP-7 threshold review instead. CONFIRM SKIP.

NYM @ PHI.reasoning: PQI and Stage agreement both support NYM (score=2) but no F-signal fires and edge sits outside the valid window, so the pipeline correctly withheld a stake. Rangel (NYM) vs McLean (PHI) confirmed live via statsapi. No live context (injury/lineup) changes the read. CONFIRM SKIP.

TEX @ ATL.reasoning: D-grade with SP xERA (-2) and PQI (-1) both opposing ATL -- the same dual-opposing-signal configuration that has been the archive's most reliable SKIP confirmation since 7/9. Eovaldi is confirmed live as TEX's probable SP today, so the SP-xERA-against-ATL read is not a data error. This is a different SP pairing from 7/17's 15-1 ATL blowout, so that specific lesson doesn't transfer, but the general D-grade dual-opposition pattern still applies. CONFIRM SKIP.

PIT @ CLE.reasoning: D-grade: Skenes (PIT) holds a real 1.17 xERA edge against the CLE pick, PQI -12.7 opposes CLE. Skenes is a legitimate ace and the model's own pick against him is exactly the kind of D-grade directional ambiguity the archive says to SKIP, not fade further. CONFIRM SKIP.

BAL @ HOU.reasoning: Mechanically clean (score=5, all four dimensions affirm, no hard caps fire), but this is the third BAL@HOU appearance in the archive and F5_bullpen_gap=+0.68 is identical to 7/17's value, where a weaker version of this same pick (grade B, BvP-driven, no SP-edge dimension) was the sole staked GOLD CONFIRM and lost 3-2. Critically, today's row carries confidence_downgrade=True and stress_warnings=bullpen_fatigue;short_term_skill -- the pipeline itself is flagging the bullpen edge (the pick's sole F-signal) as possibly a short-term/fatigue-driven blip rather than a durable skill gap. pick_prob=0.644 also sits in the home-favorite over-confidence zone (heuristic #1). DOWNGRADE to SKIP pending resolution of the bullpen-fatigue flag.

SD @ KC.reasoning: D-grade: Márquez (SD) has a real -0.73 xERA disadvantage vs Cameron (KC) baked into the score, and only Stage agreement is positive. Thin bottom-bucket pick_prob=0.531 reinforces the SKIP. CONFIRM SKIP.

MIA @ MIL.reasoning: Pre-cap A- (score=4: F-signal, SP edge 0.34 agrees, Stage agree) reduced to C(0) by HARD CAP 7 (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0185, in the same sub-0.04 range flagged as a possible false-negative zone on 7/10 and 7/18) plus HARD CAP 10. This exact MIA@MIL configuration has now appeared three straight days (7/17 DOWNGRADE with a thin 2-1 win, 7/18 CONFIRM SKIP outcome pending) without a resolved verdict on whether the caps are over-triggering here. Insufficient sample to override again today -- CONFIRM SKIP.

MIN @ CHC.reasoning: D-grade: Imanaga (CHC) confirmed live as home SP, holding a real 0.84 xERA edge against the MIN pick, with PQI also opposing (late-game-degradation, Δ=+13.8). Classic dual-opposing D-grade SKIP. CONFIRM SKIP.

CIN @ COL.reasoning: F5_bullpen_gap=+1.77 is identical to 7/17's CIN@COL value, where the same edge-just-under-4pp GOLD pick (there capped by CAP 10 at a lower pick_prob) won 7-2 and was flagged as the leading candidate for relaxing the 4pp edge floor when F5_bullpen_gap>1.5. Today's version clears cleanly at pick_prob=0.618 with no CAP 1/6/10 firing and Stage 1/2 agreeing (Δ=0.04). Genuine Hunter Greene xERA and PQI support. CONFIRM at GOLD.

WSH @ OAK.reasoning: Edge is slightly negative (-1.23pp) and PQI opposes WSH (late-game-degradation, Δ=+5.5). CAP 10 correctly zeroed the marginal-favorite pick_prob=0.542 band. Different SP pairing than 7/17's 23-4 WSH blowout, so that precedent doesn't transfer. CONFIRM SKIP.

DET @ LAA.reasoning: fair_prob=0.392 sits below the 0.42 viability floor and both SP xERA (-2) and PQI (-1) oppose LAA -- a severe dual-opposition D-grade pick. Market and quantitative signals agree the LAA pick is overstated. CONFIRM SKIP.

SF @ SEA.reasoning: fair_prob=0.355 well below the 0.42 floor, SP xERA and PQI both oppose SF, and edge=20.01pp would hit HARD CAP 6 territory if tier were elevated. 7/17's SF@SEA 7-0 win against identical opposing signals was explicitly flagged as pure variance, not a pattern to extrapolate (different SP pairing today regardless). CONFIRM SKIP.

STL @ ARI.reasoning: D-grade with SP xERA (-2) and PQI (Δ=-18.8, -1) opposing ARI. PQI opposition sits just under the 20pp threshold that drove the 7/17 STL@ARI Stage-2-vs-PQI lesson, and Stage 1/2 agree here (Δ=0.04, not the high-delta disagreement seen 7/17), so that exception doesn't directly apply -- this is a standard dual-opposing D-grade SKIP. CONFIRM SKIP.

LAD @ NYY (G2).reasoning: Makeup doubleheader nightcap; probable SPs for both NYY and LAD are unannounced in the CSV and unconfirmed on statsapi as of this run. No directional evaluation possible until SP data clears the 100-pitch Statcast gate. Game 1 LAD@NYY is tracked separately above.
